claim_audit_results.claim_summary: MAESTRO begins at TRL 2 (technology concept formulated) for all foundational research components
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase2_concept_refinement/concept_refinement_summary.json — CC-01 notes: 'TRL start point (TRL 2) is not explicitly stated in the concept brief but is consistent with the foundational nature of the research (developing new agent architectures, formal protocols, and unified memory systems from first principles). The TRL 2 start should be explicitly stated in the full proposal.' Derivation: WPs developing new AI architectures from first principles satisfy the TRL 2 definition (technology concept formulated); no source contradicts this start level.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Partner responsible assignments: ATU leads OBJ-1 and OBJ-8; BIIS leads OBJ-2; CERIA leads OBJ-3; NIHS leads OBJ-4; ISIA leads OBJ-5; ELI leads OBJ-6; BAL leads OBJ-7

Note: section text says '(BAL and FIIT)' for OBJ-7; source shows BAL as responsible and FIIT as contributing_partner — presentation acceptable, not factually incorrect.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: WP5 clinical benchmark (T5-01) will consider sex/gender as variables in the 500-case benchmark spanning 5 diagnostic domains to ensure balanced demographic representation
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier2a_instrument_schemas/extracted/section_schema_registry.json — B.1.2 field_requirements: 'Gender dimension in R&I content: how sex/gender analysis is taken into account in research and innovation content; justification if not considered relevant — mandatory except for topics identified in work programme as not requiring gender dimension integration'; docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json — T5-01: 'Clinical use case definition and diagnostic benchmark design' (NIHS-led); docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/capabilities.json — NIHS.managerial_capabilities: 'Established clinical research ethics review infrastructure'. Derivation: Tier 2A mandates gender dimension analysis → WP5 benchmark design task is the applicable locus → NIHS clinical ethics infrastructure governs → benchmark will incorporate sex/gender variables as a research content dimension.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Interdisciplinary integration across four disciplinary traditions: LLM-based AI engineering (ATU/BAL), formal methods and planning theory (ATU/CERIA), neural information retrieval and memory architectures (BIIS), and domain-applied engineering informatics (NIHS/ISIA/ELI)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/capabilities.json — all 8 partners' technical_capabilities confirm coverage of stated traditions; docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/objectives.json — pillar field shows Pillar 1 (Neuro-Symbolic Planning), Pillar 2 (Adaptive Memory), Pillar 3 (Decentralised Multi-Agent), and Demonstrators (Healthcare/Manufacturing/Logistics) mapping to four disciplinary traditions

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Strategic differentiation: integrated co-design of four capabilities (planning, memory, coordination, tool orchestration) versus three disconnected current research communities; ≥40% benchmark improvement framing for planning component
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase2_concept_refinement/concept_refinement_summary.json — strategic_differentiation field: 'MAESTRO's primary differentiator is its integrated co-design of all four capability areas... rather than independent or loosely coupled research threads... three research communities (LLM-based agents, classical planning and multi-agent systems, and memory-augmented neural systems) operate in largely disconnected tracks'; docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/objectives.json — OBJ-1 measurable_target: '≥40% improvement in plan success rate over pure LLM-based agent baselines on standardised agent benchmarks'

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Mixed-TRL profile (OUT-3 at TRL 4 while sector demonstrators reach TRL 5) is appropriate for a Research and Innovation Action
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier2a_instrument_schemas/extracted/section_schema_registry.json — B.1.1 field_requirements: 'note that expectations differ for RIAs at lower TRL versus IAs at higher TRLs'. Derivation: Tier 2A states RIAs target lower TRLs than IAs; MAESTRO's TRL 2→TRL 4/5 range falls within this expectation; OUT-3 at TRL 4 reflects a primary formal-methods contribution that does not require full operational deployment to satisfy RIA criteria.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: MAESTRO has no dedicated SSH disciplinary research pillars; SSH/ethics embedded through architectural and governance mechanisms (WP1 ethics oversight, EU AI Act auditability, WP5 clinical ethics, WP8 responsible AI assessment)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json — all 9 WPs (WP1-WP9) have technical objectives; no WP is designated as an SSH research pillar; WP1 T1-03 provides ethics compliance oversight; docs/tier2a_instrument_schemas/extracted/section_schema_registry.json — B.1.2 field_requirements: 'Social sciences and humanities integration: show role of SSH disciplines or justification for non-relevance'
